title: chamaecytisus austriacus subsp. pygmaeus
intro: Chamaecytisus austriacus subsp. pygmaeus, also known as the alpine Austrian broom, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. Chamaecytisus austriacus subsp. pygmaeus is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to take advantage of its medicinal and health-promoting properties.
